Next tonality, I call this the series of three uptones. And I used it before, and I'll use it again, where it's, you know, it's Jordan calling from sales acuity in Australia, up, up, up. Now this is something that you use all the time in sales, not just in the beginning. Let me be an example here of how a child uses it. My daughter, whenever she wanted something from me, she would use this weird tonality that all kids have, and a lot of us adults forget. And that's when they speak to you in a way where they almost assume that you actually heard it before or said it before. You never said something to them, but they'll say, like, Dad, you told me we were going for ice cream, right? You said so. And you're like, what? Did I? You actually started, like, I didn't tell them. Now you never actually said it to your child that you'll take them for ice cream or to the toy store, but they said, you said you're going to the toy store, right, Dad? You said so. And you're like, did I? Okay. And you actually get influenced and led down the path of doing something simply because someone used this series of three uptones. So again, it's very, very crucial. You'll see this actually when we go through the straight line in more detail, tactically, of exactly where you use these uptones in the scripts and how you move people into a state of absolute certainty because of it.
